title: Alpine.js Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for essential Alpine.js commands and patterns.

commands.description: Initialize Alpine.js component with reactive data
commands.scenario: Use when you need to create a reactive component with local state.

commands.description: Toggle a boolean value on click
commands.scenario: Use when you need to toggle a component's visibility or state.

commands.description: Conditionally render content based on a boolean value
commands.scenario: Use when you need to show or hide elements based on state.

commands.description: Two-way data binding with input elements
commands.scenario: Use when you need to bind input values to component data.

commands.description: Run a function when a component is initialized
commands.scenario: Use when you need to perform actions on component mount.
Use when you need to change state on mouseover.

commands.description: Dynamically bind classes based on state
commands.scenario: Use when you need to apply classes conditionally.

commands.description: Render a list of items using a loop
commands.scenario: Use when you need to iterate over a list of items in the DOM.

commands.description: Run side effects in response to reactive data changes
commands.scenario: Use when you need to perform actions when data changes.

commands.description: Conditionally disable elements based on state
commands.scenario: Use when you need to disable elements conditionally.

commands.description: Apply transitions to elements on enter
commands.scenario: Use when you need to animate elements when they enter the DOM.

commands.description: Create a reference to a DOM element
commands.scenario: Use when you need to directly interact with a DOM element.
Use when you need to dynamically apply attributes to an element.
